mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion that drives corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive returns in more than 70% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output that improves short-term earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction during tightening periods, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO changes with t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 300.0, persistent inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in more than 60% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.0, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or positive returns occur in less than 55% of triggered months over a 5-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding economic activity that lifts aggregate corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant relationship with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over consecutive 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent signals a contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization that reduces near-term earnings forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to predict return direction with accuracy exceeding 60% in out-of-sample testing.
